title: dombeya manongarivensis
intro: Dombeya manongarivensis, also known as the Manongarivo wild pear, is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It is native to Madagascar and has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Dombeya manongarivensis typically involve extracting the plant's leaves and stems to create a tea or tincture, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
